output.input: {"query":"(AI security OR prompt security OR optimization AI OR system prompt OR XML prompts) lang:en","limit":10,"mode":"Top"}
output.name: x_keyword_search

content.text:
### Top X Posts on AI Security, Prompt Security, Optimization, System Prompts, and XML Prompts (English, Top Ranked)

Here are the most relevant top posts from X (formerly Twitter) matching your query. I've focused on the highest-engagement and timely ones from early February 2026, highlighting key themes like prompt injection vulnerabilities, AI agent security, and prompt optimization.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018435053680779486)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018576960373641223)

#### Key Highlights:
1. **Agent Security & Prompt Injection Protections**:
   - SolPay announced "Agent Immune System" (AIS), a security layer for AI agents to prevent prompt injection, secret leaks, and runaway spending. Built for a Solana hackathon, it uses micropayments for instant protection.[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2019143456145265091)[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2019115480292962782)
   - Warnings about autonomous AI agents in workflows (e.g., security ops, customer support) becoming new attack surfaces via prompt injection.[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018275535697146247)

2. **Vulnerabilities in Supply Chains**:
   - Critical Docker vulnerability (#DockerDash) involving indirect prompt injection leading to RCE and data leaks. Reported by Noma Security in collaboration with Docker's team.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2018698884814393480)[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2018702065598370104)

3. **Prompt Optimization & Broader AI Security**:
   - Pydantic's guide on automating prompt optimization using GEPA, Pydantic AI, and Evals for better system prompts.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018435053680779486)
   - Emphasis that many "AI-specific" issues like prompt injection are repackaged old cybersecurity problems (e.g., injection attacks).[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018968293541925129)
   - Zypher Network's ZK-powered layer for trustless AI agents with "Proof of Prompt" for verifiable, secure behavior.[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2018576960373641223)

4. **Production AI Workflows**:
   - Shift from "vibe coding" (simple prompts) to robust agentic systems needing data foundations, security, and more.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2017407633578463526)
   - Kubernetes recommended for GenAI scaling once security, audits, and complex workflows (tool calls, approvals) kick in.[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2017708578392453211)

These posts show rising concerns around AI agent security, especially prompt injections in tools like Docker and production agents. No direct mentions of "XML prompts," but system prompt optimization ties in. If you want more details, Latest mode results, or semantic search, let me know!
